metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a persistent record of the overlay-vs-verifier contract (e.g., a CLAUDE.md section or ADR entry) so the 'domain rubric lives in the overlay' principle is discoverable independently of this plan/commit.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel, explicitly 'locked' architectural contract — domain rubric lives in the overlay, never in the verifier — but names no persistent artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, design doc) to record this decision for future contributors.
findings.location: ## What — 'per the locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier' / AC: 'Overlay remains advisory; no domain rubric leaks into code-review-verify.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief section to CLAUDE.md (or create docs/decisions/security-overlay-boundary.md) documenting: (1) the overlay-vs-verifier split, (2) what belongs in each, and (3) the rationale — then add its path to the Files list in this plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the 'domain rubric stays in the overlay' principle is documented anywhere (ADR, CLAUDE.md, or a test) today, and if not, add that documentation as a deliverable so AC-3 is verifiably enforceable.
findings.finding: The plan states 'the locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier' as if this is a pre-existing, enforced architectural contract. Reading the codebase (ADRs, CLAUDE.md, code-review-verify.md) finds no named ADR, no CLAUDE.md entry, and no automated check that enforces this boundary — it is not a 'locked' invariant in the codebase today, only a design intent this plan introduces.
findings.location: ## What section — 'locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan (or the eval spec file itself) the fixture schema for code-review overlay evals (minimum required fields per case), the scorer type and pass threshold (e.g. 'recall: at_least(6) of 8 named patterns fire; no-fire: 0 suppressions of valid patterns'), and confirm or create the eval_solver arm needed to run `rebar-code-review-security` cases.
findings.finding: The second named file ('eval spec + fixtures for the security overlay') does not exist anywhere in the repository, and the eval_solver.run_case() infrastructure has no execution arm for the `rebar-code-review-security` prompt id — meaning the eval harness needed to satisfy AC2 ('Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') must be built from scratch, but the plan never specifies the fixture schema, scorer type, or pass threshold.
findings.location: Plan § Files — 'eval spec + fixtures for the security overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'; AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define in the plan (or in the overlay spec section) the exact DET criterion id(s) that constitute the exclusion boundary (e.g. `rebar.builtin.security.secrets-gitleaks`, `high-critical-security`) and specify the suppression rule the overlay should express to the LLM (e.g. 'do not raise a finding for X if the DET criterion Y already covers it').
findings.finding: The plan references 'hard exclusions defer to gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria BY NAME' as a decision protocol for the FP-guard half of the overlay, but neither the named criteria set (which detector ids constitute the exclusion boundary) nor the suppression rule (what the LLM should do when a potential finding overlaps a DET criterion) is defined anywhere in the plan or in the current overlay file.
findings.location: Plan § What — 'Hard exclusions defer to the gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria BY NAME'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the eval harness (`eval_solver.run_case` or an alternative) has an execution arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs, or explicitly scope building that arm as part of this plan's deliverables.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' (AC-2), implying a code-review overlay eval harness already exists or needs only fixtures. Reading `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` lines 106–171 shows `run_case()` raises `ValueError` for any `prompt_id` that is not `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`, or a plan-review criterion. Code-review overlay IDs (like `rebar-code-review-security`) are none of these. No `code-review-security.eval.yaml` exists in `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`. The plan assumes eval infrastructure for code-review overlays that does not exist.
findings.location: AC-2 / Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the security overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Files/What section: extend `eval_solver.run_case` with a `code-review-*` arm (analogous to the plan-review criterion arm) that invokes the overlay runner over an inline diff fixture, so AC-2's passing eval is executable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `eval_solver.py` (or an equivalent named mechanism) to the Files list and specify how `run_case` will support `code-review-security` overlay prompt IDs, so AC2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' is runnable.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' for the security overlay, but eval_solver.run_case() has no execution arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs (it raises ValueError for any prompt_id outside completion-verifier / ticket-quality / spec-alignment / plan-review-<criterion>), and no code-review-*.eval.yaml exists anywhere in eval_specs/ or .rebar/evals/. The plan names an eval spec + fixtures as a deliverable but does not name eval_solver as a file to extend — leaving the 'passing eval' AC unsatisfiable with the current harness.
findings.location: AC2 / Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the security overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add eval_solver.py to the Files section. In eval_solver.run_case(), add a code-review overlay arm (analogous to the existing reviewer arms) that calls the four-pass code-review workflow's Pass-1 agentic runner over the fixture diff, so the eval spec's recall/no-fire cases can execute and be scored.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'dso' source: identify the concrete artifact (file path, external doc, or team standard) that defines the 8 red-team recall patterns and FP-guards, and either add it to the repo or cite it by name so the overlay content and the eval fixtures have a shared ground truth.
findings.finding: The plan's entire enrichment is predicated on 'dso content' (the source of both the 8 red-team recall patterns and the FP-guards), but 'dso' does not appear anywhere in the codebase, docs, ADRs, or CLAUDE.md — it is an unverified source assertion with no grounded artifact.
findings.location: ## What — 'Enrich the security overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` to the Files section (or explicitly acknowledge it) — it needs an execution arm for `code-review-security` (and sibling code-review overlays) to make AC2's eval runnable.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.run_case` has no execution arm for `code-review-security` (or any `code-review-*` prompt id): it handles only `completion-verifier`, `ticket-quality`, `spec-alignment`, and plan-review criterion ids, raising `ValueError` for anything else. Running the eval AC requires this consumer to be updated, but the plan does not list it.
findings.location: AC: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'; Files section (unenumerated consumer)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a quantitative pass threshold for AC2 (e.g., minimum fixture count per DSO category, recall ≥ N/8 patterns triggered, FP no-fire suite must produce 0 false positives on listed guard cases).
findings.finding: AC2's 'passing recall + no-fire eval' has no quantitative threshold for what 'passing' means, and 'does not over-suppress valid patterns' has no defined metric — an executing agent must invent the pass bar.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: AC2: 'Eval suite covers ≥1 recall fixture per DSO category (8 total) and ≥4 no-fire fixtures covering the listed FP-guards; all recall fixtures fire and all no-fire fixtures produce no finding; run via `make eval-security-overlay`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC (e.g., `make eval-security-overlay` for AC2, a `grep`/`diff` assertion for AC3, and a named file-diff or CI artifact for AC1).
findings.finding: No proving command is specified for any of the three ACs — there is no concrete command, script, or CI step that would produce evidence of completion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all three ACs) and 'Files' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the concrete file path for the security overlay eval spec (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-security.eval.yaml`) to the Files section, confirming it follows the existing `eval_specs/` convention.
findings.finding: The second named file target, 'eval spec + fixtures for the security overlay', has no concrete path in the plan and no `code-review-security.eval.yaml` (or any `code-review-*.eval.yaml`) exists anywhere in `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` — the eval spec is a hallucinated/unresolved edit target.
findings.location: Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the security overlay (recall + no-fire cases)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the complete set of rationalization-to-reject patterns the overlay must guard against (not just the one example), or explicitly state that the single named rationalization is the exhaustive list for v1.
findings.finding: The plan names 'Rationalizations-to-reject' as a class of FP-guard with only one example ('not directly exploitable => not a finding'), leaving the full vocabulary undefined. The overlay instruction cannot be complete if the set of rationalizations to reject is an open, unspecified list.
findings.location: Plan § What — 'Rationalizations-to-reject: not directly exploitable => not a finding'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the code-review-security.md change (e.g., git-revert instruction, feature-flag the enriched prompt, or document the prior prompt version as the rollback target) so a regression has a defined recovery path.
findings.finding: The plan modifies an existing file (code-review-security.md) changing the live security overlay's behavior, but provides no rollback path or back-out plan if the enriched prompt causes regressions in production use.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If the enriched overlay regresses recall or triggers false-suppression in production, revert code-review-security.md to the prior commit (git revert <sha>). The eval spec pinned in this plan serves as the gate; do not merge unless both recall and no-fire checks pass.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify the choice to embed recall patterns + FP-guards inside the Pass-1 prompt rather than using the existing project-supplied-criteria seam (ADRs 0015–0017 / criteria_routing.json), or explicitly note that this is complementary and explain why both mechanisms are needed.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel first-instance pattern — embedding structured recall patterns + FP-discrimination rubric directly inside a Pass-1 LLM overlay prompt — but provides no justification for why this approach is chosen over the existing project-supplied-criteria mechanism (ADRs 0015–0017, `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`), which already provides a formal seam for injecting domain rubric into the review gate.
findings.location: ## What — 'locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier' / Files: src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-security.md

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
